steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply the Exterior Angle Theorem
steps.content:
When a side of a triangle is extended, the exterior angle equals the sum of the two remote interior angles. Side QR is extended through R to S, making angle PRS an exterior angle.
$$\angle PRS = \angle P + \angle Q$$
$$(x+y) = (4x+9) + (5x+4)$$
$$x + y = 9x + 13$$
$$y = 8x + 13$$

steps.title: Set Up the Triangle Angle Sum
steps.content:
The three interior angles of triangle PQR must sum to 180 degrees.
$$(4x+9) + (5x+4) + (5y+4) = 180$$
$$9x + 17 + 5y = 180$$
$$9x + 5y = 163$$

steps.title: Solve the System of Equations
steps.content:
Substitute $y = 8x + 13$ from the first equation into $9x + 5y = 163$:
$$9x + 5(8x + 13) = 163$$
$$9x + 40x + 65 = 163$$
$$49x = 98$$
$$x = 2$$
Then $y = 8(2) + 13 = 29$.

Therefore $x + y = 2 + 29 = 31$.
